compiled_state.title: As a developer or non-technical user, I begin onboarding with a comfort assessment that auto-builds a confidence context
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add a pre-flight phase that runs before the current Phase 1 auto-detection. The phase asks a single comfort-level question (technical/non-technical), runs detect-stack.sh and project-detect.sh, assigns confidence levels (high/medium/low) per dimension, and builds a structured confidence context object in the scratchpad.

**Why**: Foundation for the entire epic. S2 and S3 cannot function without a stable confidence context schema. All downstream routing and document integration read from this object.

**Scope**:
- IN: comfort level question (first interaction), detect-stack.sh + project-detect.sh → confidence level assignment for all 7 understanding areas (stack, commands, arch, infra, CI, design, enforcement), confidence context schema definition, schema contract doc in plugins/dso/docs/contracts/, scratchpad init
- OUT: document folder scan (S2), routing logic (S3), batch scripts (S4), hook sequencing (S5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the first interaction in an onboarding session is a comfort question (technical/non-technical) before any auto-detection output is shown
  <- Satisfies: "A pre-flight phase runs before any onboarding questions: user's comfort level is assessed"
- When this story is complete, the scratchpad contains a structured confidence context object with a per-dimension confidence level (high/medium/low) for all 7 understanding areas, derived from detect-stack.sh and project-detect.sh output
  <- Satisfies: "a confidence context object is built — routing all subsequent questions"
- When this story is complete, a confidence context schema contract document exists in plugins/dso/docs/contracts/ specifying all field names, types, valid confidence levels, and rules for how S2/S3 may update the object
  <- Satisfies: "a confidence context object is built — routing all subsequent questions"
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for the confidence level assignment logic (e.g., node-npm output -> high, unknown output -> low)
  <- Satisfies: "A pre-flight phase runs before any onboarding questions"

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Schema contract is consumed by S2 and S3 — changes after this story is merged require coordinating updates in both downstream stories
- [Reliability] Stack detection false positive risk: stray package.json in subdirectory could elevate to high confidence; surface detected values to user even on high confidence where wrong config impact is severe
- [Testing] Skill file changes require scripted session replay to verify — implementation plan must define a fixture-based verification method

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate when blocked — proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
